# Aura OmniMesh

> A local-first, serverless peer-to-peer exchange protocol. Phones discover
> each other over Bluetooth / local Wi-Fi / LAN, publish signed barter
> intents ("offer: guitar lessons", "need: help moving"), and an on-device
> multilingual ML model (MiniLM) finds closed multilateral barter rings —
> A gives to B, B to C, C back to A — with no internet, no servers, no
> accounts, and no tokens.

## Key facts
- Zero servers by design: all state lives in an embedded on-device database;
  devices sync via CRDT operation logs signed with per-device Ed25519 keys.
- Multilingual semantic matching (English + Russian) runs fully on-device;
  published text never reaches any third party service.
- Ring lifecycle: discover -> lock (every participant signs) -> confirmed ->
  fulfilled; withdrawal breaks the ring visibly for everyone.
- Reputation is computed locally from signed completed-exchange history and
  is never accepted from the wire.
- Platforms: Android, iOS, and a web PWA light client that pairs with a
  nearby core node via QR code over the LAN.
- Free, no ads, no analytics, no payment processing. Version 1 is pure
  barter of goods, services, and time.

## Pages
- / : product overview, how it works, FAQ
- /about/ : full protocol explanation — what it is, why it exists, how it
  works (6 steps), what it solves, who it is for, design principles, and
  explicit non-goals. The canonical long-form description of the project.
- /privacy.html : privacy policy (EN/RU) — nothing is collected
- /terms.html : terms of service (EN/RU) — discovery tool, not a broker

## Languages
The About section is fully translated; each language is a distinct URL with
reciprocal hreflang alternates (see /sitemap.xml):
- /about/ (en, x-default), /about/es/, /about/ru/, /about/uk/, /about/ko/,
  /about/zh/, /about/pt/, /about/hi/, /about/id/, /about/ja/

## Web light client
- /app/ : the browser PWA light client (Flutter web). Joins a nearby core
  node over the LAN; the on-device ML model is served same-origin from
  /model/ (a proxy to the pinned release asset).
